Talent Relations — Standard operating procedure

Employee Retention Framework

A standardized, evidence-based approach to employee retention and compensation decisions.

Purpose Escalation trigger Process Roles Guiding principle Video Resources
Framework overview
What
MoonTech's standard decision model for retention and compensation exceptions.
Objective
Ensure every retention decision is fair, consistent and evidence-based.
Scope
Counter-offer rejected Compensation ask exceeds the 30% cap
Decision Principles
Evidence over opinion Consistency over exceptions Business impact first Internal pay equity Document every decision
Result
Every case follows one documented evaluation, approval and communication process, ensuring consistent decisions, protected pay equity and a complete audit trail.

Escalation trigger

TR gets involved when either condition is met:

  • The employee has rejected a counter-offer, or
  • The required compensation adjustment exceeds 30%

TR acknowledges the referral within 4 hours. Cases are submitted via the Workflow shortcut in #talent-retention.

Process

1
Evidence build
48-72 hrs
2
Manager briefing
Before decision
3
TR decision
24 hrs
4
Negotiation
5 working days
5
Closure
24 hrs

Typical end-to-end turnaround: 8-10 working days from case submission to closure, depending on business approvals, evidence collection and employee response timelines.

The process below outlines the standard workflow followed for every retention case, including ownership, expected timelines and required evidence at each stage.

Step 1: Evidence build

SLA: 48 to 72 hrs, TR-led
Market benchmarking (external)
  • External comp data (LinkedIn, Glassdoor, surveys)
  • Role scarcity / demand signal
  • Competitive offer context, if known
Internal evidence review
  • Performance evidence (delivery, outcomes, consistency)
  • Business impact & role criticality
  • Replaceability assessment

Step 2: Manager briefing

SLA: Before decision is issued

TR presents the case across three dimensions, each scored High / Medium / Low:

DimensionWhat's assessed
Business ImpactRole criticality, project dependency, manager dependency risk, business continuity impact, replaceability difficulty
Retention RiskExternal offer presence, market compensation gap, performance level, engagement concerns
Replacement CostTime to hire, ramp-up time, knowledge depth/specialization, market scarcity of role

Step 3: TR decision

SLA: 24 hrs post-evidence
DecisionKey factorsAction
ExitPerformance gaps, cultural misfit, high replacement cost acceptableTR initiates exit process → KM handover assigned → dignified exit process → case file closed
RetainHigh value, strong fit, positive retention ROISubmit for TD budget approval → TR prepares pre-approved offer → business leader signs off → TR presents offer → case file closed
30% cap override: all three required.
  • Market pay analysis confirms a documented gap above 30%
  • Business impact score supports the override
  • Team leader formally approves in writing before TR presents to the employee
Compression risk to peers in the same band must be assessed. If High, escalate to the Talent Director (TD) before proceeding.

Step 4: Retention negotiation

SLA: Within 5 working days
  • Pre-approved compensation only
  • Structured negotiation only

Employee decision window: 48 hours max, then final closure is required.

Step 5: Closure & governance

SLA: 24 hrs post-decision
  • ☐ Document everything in order as it happens
  • ☐ Schedule and conduct a 90-day follow-up review if retained
  • ☐ Execute complete exit processing procedures if not retained

Roles & responsibilities

RoleResponsibility
Talent Relations (TR)Owns evidence-building, decision recommendation, negotiation, and closure documentation. Owns the quality of the recommendation, not the final business decision.
Manager / Business LeaderProvides dependency/context input; signs off on retain offers; formally approves 30%-cap overrides in writing.
Talent Director (TD)Approves retention budget; escalation point when compression risk is High.

Guiding principle

Choose the path with the strongest long-term business value. Balance immediate costs against sustainable talent strategy outcomes. TR challenges the leader's position when data supports a different outcome. Agreement is not the goal; the right outcome is.
